subjectVariations: Children's Rights Violations in Iran - Documented Pattern of Violence by Islamic Republic
Urgent Action Needed: Systematic Abuse of Minors During Iran Protests
Iran Convention Violations: Violence Against Children Demands International Response
Child Protection Crisis: Iranian Regime's Documented Attacks on Minors
Grave Violations Against Children in Iran - UN Investigation Required
Documented State Violence Against Minors in Iran - UNICEF Action Needed
Islamic Republic of Iran 2026: Systematic Pattern of Child Rights Violations
ttpTemplate.body:
I am writing to you regarding the killings and abductions of children by the Islamic Republic regime in Iran, and request that your office draw urgent attention to a documented pattern of grave violations against children in Iran during nationwide protests in January 2026.

The killings of children display only a fraction of the atrocities being committed by the government of Iran against its own people. The actions of the Islamic Republic demonstrate a pattern of state violence against minors, including children shot while walking with family members, sitting on a parent's shoulders, or travelling in vehicles.

Multiple human rights organizations and monitoring bodies have confirmed that hundreds of children have been killed during the protest crackdown. The Human Rights Activists News Agency (HRANA), the primary civil-society tracker of protest-related deaths, reports that at least 207 children under the age of 18 are among the verified deaths.[1] The Coordination Council of Iranian Teachers' Trade Associations has separately reported that between 163 and 200 school students and children were killed, with a significant concentration during the January 8–9 crackdown.[2] Other international organizations, including Amnesty International, Human Rights Watch, and other UN bodies, have confirmed the killing of children through the use of lethal force against minors, even where they do not publish consolidated child-specific totals.[3]

Across the documented cases and corroborating reporting:
- children were killed by live ammunition or pellet rounds fired by the Islamic Republic's so-called security forces;
- in multiple instances, families of the victims reported delays or denials of medical care, exacerbated by nationwide communications shutdowns; and
- families of the victims were subjected to post-incident intimidation, including denial of bodies, payment of "bullet fees", pressure to pay for the release of remains, falsification of causes of death, and harassment during burials.

In addition to the documented killings, credible international reporting confirms the widespread illegal abduction and detention of children in January 2026. Children, some as young as twelve, were reportedly arrested without warrants, are detained in undisclosed locations, denied access to legal counsel, and subject to threats and physical mistreatment.[4] These abductions were often carried out as part of broader security sweeps and, in some cases, appear to have been used to punish or silence families affected by protest-related violence. The arrest and detention of children under such conditions further demonstrate a systematic pattern of abuse and violence against children, extending beyond lethal force to include arbitrary deprivation of liberty and denial of due process. These findings are consistent with statements made by the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights, who in January 2026 publicly called on Iranian authorities to end their "brutal" repression, citing the use of live ammunition against civilians, the obstruction of information through communications shutdowns, and the absence of accountability for serious human rights violations committed during this period.[5]

The killings and mass arrests described above were carried out by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps and its affiliated militia ("IRGC"), a military and security force that operates directly under the authority of the Iranian state. The IRGC is formally designated as a terrorist organization by Canada, the United States, and the European Union. The violence and repression carried out by the IRGC constitute not only grave human rights violations, but acts of terror perpetrated by a state-backed entity against unarmed civilians, including children.

Iran is a State Party to the Convention on the Rights of the Child, which guarantees the right to life, protection from all forms of violence, freedom from arbitrary detention, and access to medical care and legal safeguards.[6] The killings of children, together with the widespread abduction and detention of children raises serious concerns regarding Iran's compliance with these binding obligations, and must be stopped.

The mandates of the UN Committee on the Rights of the Child and UNICEF arise directly from the Convention on the Rights of the Child and UN General Assembly resolutions tasking them with child protection advocacy. The killing of children and denial of medical care directly undermine UNICEF's core mandate to protect children's survival and development and justify urgent engagement and advocacy.

We submit that making announcements condemning the regime's conduct is no longer and we ask that your offices take meaningful action by doing the following:
- initiate an independent investigation into the killings, arrests, and detention of children during the January 2026 protests;
- publicly condemn the use of lethal force, abductions and arbitrary detention against minors;
- call for the protection of families, witnesses, and sources from retaliation and intimidation;
- establish a task force to document and preserve the evidence related to the crimes committed by the Islamic Republic against children;
- ensure that these cases are preserved for future accountability mechanisms, including international and universal jurisdiction processes;
- deploy emergency teams to secure the immediate release of the detained children and facilitate their safe return to their families; and
- report these human rights violations to the UN Security Council.

The killing of children, the arbitrary and illegal abduction of minors, and the intimidation of families cannot be normalized or ignored. There are lives on the line, and time is of the essence.

Respectfully, 



[1] Human Rights Activists News Agency (HRANA), Day 43 of the Protests: Pressure and Threats on Universities and Political Activists (online), January 2026, online: https://www.en-hrana.org/day-43-of-the-protests-pressure-and-threats-on-universities-and-political-activists
[2] Coordination Council of Iranian Teachers' Trade Associations, reported in Iran International, February 2026, online: https://www.iranintl.com/en/202602052916
[3] Amnesty International, Iran: Authorities Unleash Heavily Militarized Clampdown to Hide Protest Massacres (23 January 2026), online: https://www.amnesty.org/en/latest/news/2026/01/iran-authorities-unleash-heavily-militarized-clampdown-to-hide-protest-massacres/
[4] Farnaz Fassihi, Iran Arrests Children as Protests Continue, New York Times (30 January 2026), online: How Iran Crushed a Citizen Uprising With Lethal Force - The New York Times
[5] Volker Türk, High Commissioner Calls on Iranian Authorities to End Their Brutal Repression (23 January 2026), Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights, online: https://www.ohchr.org/en/statements-and-speeches/2026/01/high-commissioner-turk-calls-iranian-authorities-end-their-brutal
[6] Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights, Status of Ratification – Convention on the Rights of the Child, UN Treaty Body Database (13 July 1994), online: https://tbinternet.ohchr.org/_layouts/15/TreatyBodyExternal/Treaty.aspx?CountryID=81&Lang=en.
